01_initial_request.target_name: Large-scale protein machines (SARS-CoV-2 spike protein trimer, ATP citrate lyase tetramer, and P4-ATPases)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To extend the application of a coarse-grained model to analyze conformational pathways and working mechanisms of large-scale protein systems
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Conformational free energy profiles (energy change and energy barrier)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Static crystal structures of large-scale protein machines are available from PDB database. [Gap] Missing parts in structures need to be repaired and systems need to be prepared for simulation. [Decision] To achieve complete molecular models, chose to use Modeller software to perform structure repair on target proteins, expecting to obtain complete endpoint assemblies.

parameters.processing: Missing parts repaired, all ligands removed, proteins trimmed to same length

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Complete endpoint assemblies connecting specific biological processes were built based on crystal structures from Protein Data Bank database

references.citation: Webb, B.; Sali, A. Comparative Protein Structure Modeling Using MODELLER. Curr. Protoc. Bioinform. 2016, 54, 5.6.1-5.6.37.
references.relevance: Provides methodology for protein structure modeling and repair

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial endpoint structures are now complete. [Gap] Conformational pathways between different endpoint structures need to be constructed to sample intermediate conformations. [Decision] To achieve conformational pathway sampling, chose to use Targeted Molecular Dynamics (TMD) to perform conformational transition simulation on protein systems, expecting to obtain series of intermediate conformations representing the transition process.

parameters.restraint_type: Harmonic restraints on backbone heavy atoms
parameters.alignment: Initial structure aligned to target structure using backbone heavy atoms
parameters.prevention: System restrained throughout simulation to prevent abnormal translation and rotation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Intermediate structures of the whole transition process were obtained by interchanging initial and final states after TMD completion

references.citation: Schlitter, J.; Engels, M.; Krüger, P. Targeted molecular dynamics: A new approach for searching pathways of conformational transitions. J. Mol. Graph. 1994, 12, 84-89.
references.relevance: Provides methodology for targeted molecular dynamics simulation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Intermediate conformations along conformational pathways have been sampled. [Gap] Accurate electrostatic charges need to be computed for each structure to evaluate CG free energy. [Decision] To achieve accurate charge determination, chose to use Monte Carlo Proton Transfer (MCPT) method to perform ionization state calculation on protein residues, expecting to obtain converged electrostatic interaction states.

parameters.process: Proton transfer attempts between pairs of ionizable residues or within ionizable residue and bulk solvent
parameters.convergence: Repeated until electrostatic interaction of folded protein converges

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ionization states of protein residues were obtained to evaluate CG free energy

references.citation: Beroza, P.; Fredkin, D.R.; Okamura, M.Y.; Feher, G. Protonation of interacting residues in a protein by a Monte Carlo method. Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 1991, 88, 5804-5808.
references.relevance: Provides methodology for Monte Carlo proton transfer calculations

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Electrostatic charges have been determined for all intermediate structures. [Gap] Conformational free energy profiles need to be calculated using the CG model. [Decision] To achieve free energy evaluation, chose to use Molaris-XG software to perform coarse-grained free energy calculation on protein structures, expecting to obtain conformational free energy profiles.

parameters.energy_terms: Sidechain van der Waals, mainchain solvation, mainchain hydrogen bond, sidechain electrostatic, polar, hydrophobic, mainchain/sidechain van der Waals
parameters.membrane_proteins: Membrane particles added, molecular dynamics relaxation carried out for energy convergence

02_agent_trajectory.observation: CG free energy was calculated to obtain conformational free energy profiles for all three protein systems

references.citation: Lee, F.S.; Chu, Z.T.; Warshel, A. Microscopic and semimicroscopic calculations of electrostatic energies in proteins by the POLARIS and ENZYMIX programs. J. Comput. Chem. 1993, 14, 161-185.
references.relevance: Provides methodology for electrostatic energy calculations in Molaris-XG

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CG free energy calculations completed for soluble proteins. [Gap] P4-ATPase is a transmembrane protein requiring membrane environment modeling. [Decision] To achieve accurate membrane protein simulation, chose to use Molaris-XG software to perform membrane modeling and energy convergence on P4-ATPase system, expecting to obtain membrane-embedded protein models with converged energies.

parameters.membrane_model: Hydrophobic contributions scaled down by factor of ~3.6
parameters.polar_contribution: Does not consider polar contribution of sidechains
parameters.optimization: CG model optimized for membrane proteins

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Membrane particles were added and molecular dynamics relaxation was carried out to attain energy convergence for P4-ATPase system

references.citation: Vorobyov, I.; Kim, I.; Chu, Z.T.; Warshel, A. Refining the treatment of membrane proteins by coarse-grained models. Proteins 2016, 84, 92-117.
references.relevance: Provides methodology for membrane protein treatment in CG models

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Free energy profiles have been calculated for all three protein systems. [Gap] Energy barriers and energy changes need to be analyzed to provide mechanistic explanations. [Decision] To achieve mechanistic understanding, chose to use CG model energy analysis to perform conformational pathway interpretation on spike protein, ACLY, and P4-ATPase systems, expecting to obtain biological mechanism explanations.

parameters.systems_analyzed: SARS-CoV-2 spike protein trimer, ATP citrate lyase tetramer, P4-ATPases
parameters.metrics: Energy change and energy barrier between endpoint structures
parameters.pathways: Multiple conformational pathways constructed for ACLY (coupled and cascade)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Energy profiles provided reasonable mechanism explanations for RBD opening of spike protein, phospholipid transportation of P4-ATPase, and loop translocation of ACLY

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Coarse-Grained (CG) Model Free Energy Calculation with Conformational Pathway Analysis

spike_protein_energy_barrier.interpretation: Energy barrier of spike trimer's conformational transition from S-closed to S-open state

spike_protein_free_energy_difference.interpretation: Energy difference between E and E' states for ACLY (note: this value appears in ACLY section but represents conformational energy change)

ACLY_pathway1_barrier1.interpretation: First energy barrier in coupled pathway for ACLY substrate-binding process

ACLY_pathway1_barrier2.interpretation: Second energy barrier in coupled pathway for ACLY substrate-binding process

ACLY_pathway2_barrier1.interpretation: Energy barrier of E→T1 in cascade pathway 2 for ACLY

ACLY_pathway3_barrier2.interpretation: Energy barrier of T2→E' in cascade pathway 3 for ACLY

P4_ATPase_barrier1.interpretation: Energy barrier for transition from E1P-ADP to E1P in P4-ATPase lipid transfer cycle

P4_ATPase_barrier2.interpretation: Energy barrier for transition from E2P to E2Pi-PL in P4-ATPase lipid transfer cycle

P4_ATPase_E1P_energy.interpretation: Highest energy state of E1P suggesting instability in observed conformation

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was successfully achieved. The CG model was effectively extended to analyze conformational pathways of large-scale protein systems including SARS-CoV-2 spike protein trimer, ATP citrate lyase tetramer, and P4-ATPases. Conformational free energy profiles with quantifiable energy changes and energy barriers were obtained for all three systems, providing reasonable mechanistic explanations for their biological processes. The CG model demonstrated effectiveness in depicting energy profiles of conformational pathways between endpoint structures, especially for large-scale systems.
